The attached spreadsheet shows the inventory for a movie and video game rental store in Seattle, Washington. What is the title of the oldest Blu-Ray recorded in this spreadsheet? Return it as appearing in the spreadsheet.


To answer this request we have assembled the following team:

WebSurfer: A helpful assistant with access to a web browser. Ask them to perform web searches, open pages, and interact with content (e.g., clicking links, scrolling the viewport, filling in form fields, etc.). It can also summarize the entire page, or answer questions based on the content of the page. It can also be asked to sleep and wait for pages to load, in cases where the page seems not yet fully loaded.
FileSurfer: An agent that can handle local files.
Coder: A helpful and general-purpose AI assistant that has strong language skills, Python skills, and Linux command line skills.
Executor: A computer terminal that performs no other action than running Python scripts (provided to it quoted in ```python code blocks), or sh shell scripts (provided to it quoted in ```sh code blocks).


Here is an initial fact sheet to consider:

1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS  
- The file in question is located at: /NHNHOME/WORKSPACE/0426030030_A/jungwoonshin/tips_project/dataset/gaia/files/2023/validation/32102e3e-d12a

Please write a Python script that reads the Excel file located at '/NHNHOME/WORKSPACE/0426030030_A/jungwoonshin/tips_project/dataset/gaia/files/2023/validation/32102e3e-d12a-4209-9163-7b3a104efe5d.xlsx', locates the 'Blu-Ray' section, extracts the 'Title' and 'Year' columns for entries under that section (before 'Video Games'), and finds the title with the minimum (earliest) year. Return only the title exactly as it appears in the spreadsheet.

```python
import pandas as pd

# Load the Excel file
file_path = '/NHNHOME/WORKSPACE/0426030030_A/jungwoonshin/tips_project/dataset/gaia/files/2023/validation/32102e3e-d12a-4209-9163-7b3a104efe5d.xlsx'
df = pd.read_excel(file_path, header=None)  # Read without headers to capture all data

# Locate the start of the Blu-Ray section
blu_ray_idx = df[df[0] == 'Blu-Ray'].index
if len(blu_ray_idx) == 0:
    raise ValueError("Blu-Ray section not found in the file.")
blu_ray_start = blu_ray_idx[0] + 1  # Data starts after the header

# Locate the start of the Video Games section
video_games_idx = df[df[0] == 'Video Games'].index
if len(video_games_idx) == 0:
    raise ValueError("Video Games section not found in the file.")
video_games_start = video_games_idx[0]

# Extract the Blu-Ray section
blu_ray_section = df.iloc[blu_ray_start:video_games_start].copy()

# The first row contains the column headers
blu_ray_section.columns = df.iloc[blu_ray_start - 1]  # Set header from row above section
blu_ray_section = blu_ray_section.dropna(subset=['Title'])  # Remove any fully empty rows

# Ensure 'Year' is numeric
blu_ray_section['Year'] = pd.to_numeric(blu_ray_section['Year'], errors='coerce')

# Drop rows where Year is NaN
blu_ray_section = blu_ray_section.dropna(subset=['Year'])

# Find the title with the minimum year
oldest_title = blu_ray_section.loc[blu_ray_section['Year'].idxmin(), 'Title']

# Print only the title
print(oldest_title)
```
Please run the provided Python script to extract the title of the oldest Blu-Ray from the spreadsheet and return the result.

Revise the script to assign generic column names (e.g., 0, 1, 2, 3, 4) and extract the title from column 0 and year from column 2. Then find the row with the minimum year and return the corresponding title.

```python
import pandas as pd

# Load the Excel file
file_path = '/NHNHOME/WORKSPACE/0426030030_A/jungwoonshin/tips_project/dataset/gaia/files/2023/validation/32102e3e-d12a-4209-9163-7b3a104efe5d.xlsx'
df = pd.read_excel(file_path, header=None)  # Read without headers to capture all data

# Locate the start of the Blu-Ray section
blu_ray_idx = df[df[0] == 'Blu-Ray'].index
if len(blu_ray_idx) == 0:
    raise ValueError("Blu-Ray section not found in the file.")
blu_ray_start = blu_ray_idx[0] + 1  # Data starts after the header

# Locate the start of the Video Games section
video_games_idx = df[df[0] == 'Video Games'].index
if len(video_games_idx) == 0:
    raise ValueError("Video Games section not found in the file.")
video_games_start = video_games_idx[0]

# Extract the Blu-Ray section
blu_ray_section = df.iloc[blu_ray_start:video_games_start].copy()

# Assign generic column names
blu_ray_section.columns = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]

# Extract Title (column 0) and Year (column 2)
blu_ray_section['Title'] = blu_ray_section[0]
blu_ray_section['Year'] = pd.to_numeric(blu_ray_section[2], errors='coerce')

# Drop rows where Year is NaN
blu_ray_section = blu_ray_section.dropna(subset=['Year'])

# Find the title with the minimum year
oldest_title = blu_ray_section.loc[blu_ray_section['Year'].idxmin(), 'Title']

# Print only the title
print(oldest_title)
```
